source_verification_note: industry_sources entries indicate the published works that informed or directionally support each lever's savings range. Where no external benchmark was found, INTERNAL_CALIBRATION is noted. All cited sources are real publications; specific savings percentages remain internal calibrations unless the source directly cites a matching range.

condition_precedents: Claims data quality validated (> 90% structured fields, < 5% exception rate)
AI triage model trained and back-tested on ≥ 2 years of historical claims
IRDAI / regulatory approval obtained for automated settlement thresholds
industry_sources: IRDAI — Annual Report 2023-24 (irdai.gov.in)
McKinsey & Company — Why Zero-Based Budgeting Makes Sense Again (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/why-zero-based-budgeting-makes-sense-again)
Gartner — IT Budget Benchmarks 2024 (gartner.com/en/information-technology/insights/it-budget)

execution_playbook.step: Map claims journey by product line: measure FNOL-to-settlement cycle time, manual touchpoints, and STP rate by claim type and value band
Design STP pathway by claim type: auto-adjudication rules for low-value/low-complexity claims; integrate with surveyor and hospital network
Deploy FNOL automation (WhatsApp/IVR), triage scoring model, and digital document collection; eliminate manual data entry at intake
Track STP rate, claims processing cost per claim, and customer satisfaction (NPS/CSAT) by product line monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Claims STP rate <30% for product lines where IRDAI allows straight-through processing (motor own-damage, health cashless)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: STP implementation will materially reduce cost per claim and settlement time

diagnostic_signals.signal: Claims processing cost per claim >₹800 for motor or >₹1,200 for health claims vs peer benchmark
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation will reduce claims processing cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Average claims settlement time >15 days for cashless health or >30 days for motor own-damage
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Process automation will improve settlement speed and customer satisfaction

required_data_fields: Claims journey map with volume, STP rate, and TAT by product line and claim type
Claims processing cost per claim by product (FTE cost + surveyor + system)
FNOL data: channel mix (agent, digital, call centre) and data completeness at intake
Policy admin system and claims management system integration architecture
IRDAI compliance requirements for automated adjudication

condition_precedents: Underwriting rules engine updated and tested for STP eligibility thresholds
Actuarial sign-off on automated risk scoring models
Regulatory compliance review for algorithmic underwriting decisions completed
industry_sources: IRDAI — Annual Report 2023-24 (irdai.gov.in)
Gartner — IT Budget Benchmarks 2024 (gartner.com/en/information-technology/insights/it-budget)
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Map underwriting decision workflow by product line: measure time-to-quote, referral rate, and loss ratio by underwriting tier
Build rules-based auto-acceptance engine for standard risks; integrate external data (bureau, weather, geo-risk, IoT) for risk enrichment
Deploy AI risk scoring model for SME and commercial lines; run parallel with manual underwriting for validation
Track auto-acceptance rate, referral rate, combined ratio by segment, and underwriting team productivity monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Underwriting referral rate >40% of new business quotes requiring senior underwriter review
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Rules engine and AI scoring will reduce referral rate and speed up quote turnaround

diagnostic_signals.signal: Time-to-quote for standard commercial lines >5 days vs competitor benchmark of <24 hours
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation will improve conversion rate through faster quote turnaround

diagnostic_signals.signal: Loss ratio variance >10 percentage points between auto-accepted and manually-underwritten risks of similar profile
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Data enrichment and AI scoring will improve risk selection quality

required_data_fields: Underwriting workflow data: quote volume, referral rate, TAT, and accept/decline rate
Loss ratio by product line, underwriting tier, and channel
External data availability for risk enrichment (bureau, geo-risk, IoT sensors)
Policy admin system architecture and API capability
IRDAI compliance requirements for automated underwriting

condition_precedents: Core policy admin system (PAS) supports digital workflows
e-signature capability enabled and legally validated
Customer communication migrated to digital channels (> 70% penetration target set)
industry_sources: IRDAI — Annual Report 2023-24 (irdai.gov.in)
Ardent Partners — AP Metrics That Matter 2024 (ardentpartners.com/ap-metrics-that-matter-in-2025/)
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Audit policy lifecycle touchpoints: issuance, endorsement, renewal, and lapse processes; measure manual effort and error rate per transaction type
Consolidate PAS (Policy Administration System) to single platform where multiple systems operate; map integration with CRM, billing, and regulatory reporting
Launch digital self-service for policyholders: endorsements, premium payment, and document download via app/portal; automate renewal trigger
Track digital self-service adoption rate, policy issuance TAT, and operations cost per policy in force monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Policy issuance or endorsement TAT >24 hours for standard products vs digital-first insurer benchmark of <2 hours
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Digitisation will improve speed and reduce manual cost per policy

diagnostic_signals.signal: Operations cost per policy in force >₹1,200/year vs peer benchmark <₹800, indicating manual-heavy model
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Self-service and PAS consolidation will reduce unit cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Multiple PAS platforms (>2) creating data inconsistency and integration overhead
diagnostic_signals.confirms: PAS consolidation will reduce IT maintenance cost and data quality issues

required_data_fields: Policy lifecycle transaction volumes and TAT by type (issuance, endorsement, renewal, lapse)
Operations cost per policy by transaction type and product
PAS landscape and integration architecture
Digital channel adoption rate (app, web portal) by policyholder segment
IRDAI compliance requirements for digital policy issuance and documentation

condition_precedents: Historical fraud case data labelled and validated (> 3 years, minimum 1,000 confirmed fraud cases)
ML fraud scoring integrated into claims intake workflow
Special Investigations Unit (SIU) capacity scaled to handle referrals
industry_sources: IRDAI — Annual Report 2023-24 (irdai.gov.in)
McKinsey & Company — Why Zero-Based Budgeting Makes Sense Again (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/why-zero-based-budgeting-makes-sense-again)
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Analyse historical claims for fraud indicators: claims made just after policy inception, late-reported high-value claims, and repeat claimants
Build ML fraud scoring model using claims, policy, and network data; validate precision and recall on holdout dataset
Integrate fraud score into claims triage workflow: flag high-risk claims for investigation before settlement; automate low-risk claims
Track fraud detected as % of claims value, false-positive investigation rate, and fraud loss reduction quarterly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Claims fraud loss >1% of net earned premium for motor or health lines, indicating inadequate detection
diagnostic_signals.confirms: AI fraud detection will improve detection rate and reduce fraud loss

diagnostic_signals.signal: Investigation team >20 FTEs investigating all flagged claims with high false-positive rate (>70%)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: ML scoring will improve investigation efficiency and reduce false positives

diagnostic_signals.signal: No fraud scoring model—all fraud detection relies on adjuster judgment with no systematic analytics
diagnostic_signals.confirms: AI model introduction will improve detection systematically

required_data_fields: Historical claims data with confirmed fraud labels (≥2 years, ≥5,000 confirmed fraud cases)
Policy, customer, and network data for feature engineering
Investigation case outcomes and false-positive rate
Claims management system architecture for scoring integration
IRDAI compliance requirements for AI-based fraud detection

condition_precedents: Channel cost and profitability analysis completed per product line
Digital direct and bancassurance channel capability built or contracted
Agent / broker rationalisation plan agreed with sales leadership
industry_sources: IRDAI — Annual Report 2023-24 (irdai.gov.in)
McKinsey & Company — Why Zero-Based Budgeting Makes Sense Again (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/why-zero-based-budgeting-makes-sense-again)
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Analyse distribution cost by channel (tied agents, brokers, bancassurance, digital, direct): commission, acquisition cost, and persistency by channel
Build customer lifetime value model by channel: compare 13th and 25th month persistency, premium size, and claim frequency
Rebalance channel investment towards higher-CLV, lower-cost channels; renegotiate commission structures with brokers and banks
Track new business premium by channel, cost per new policy, and persistency ratio monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Distribution cost (commission + acquisition) >25% of first-year premium for retail life or >15% for general insurance
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Channel mix rebalancing towards lower-commission channels will improve expense ratio

diagnostic_signals.signal: 13th month persistency ratio <70% for agent-sourced policies vs >85% for digital-sourced policies
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Shifting channel mix towards digital will improve LTV and reduce policy lapse cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Digital channel contributing <10% of new business despite comparable unit economics
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Digital channel investment will improve acquisition cost without sacrificing quality

required_data_fields: New business premium and policy count by channel (12 months)
Commission rate and acquisition cost by channel and product
Persistency data (13th and 25th month) by channel
Claims frequency and loss ratio by channel
Digital channel funnel data (leads, conversion, policy issued)

condition_precedents: Actuarial review of optimal retention levels completed
Treaty renewal strategy agreed with reinsurance broker
Risk appetite board approval obtained for changed retention
industry_sources: IRDAI — Annual Report 2023-24 (irdai.gov.in)
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Review treaty structure vs actual loss experience: compare cedant strategy (proportional vs XL), retention levels, and RI panel performance
Benchmark RI cost (net RI premium as % of gross) vs peer insurers; model optimised retention and treaty structure using stochastic loss model
Run competitive RI tender with shortlisted panel; negotiate improved terms for proportional treaties and aggregate XL covers
Track RI cost ratio, net combined ratio, and RI recovery rate by treaty annually; submit updated IRDAI filing

diagnostic_signals.signal: RI cost as % of gross premium >25% for lines where peer average is <18%, indicating over-cession
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Retention optimisation and treaty restructuring will reduce RI cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Proportional RI cession >40% for motor or fire where historical combined ratio supports higher self-retention
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Shifting to XL or higher retention will reduce cession cost on profitable lines

diagnostic_signals.signal: RI panel not reviewed for >3 years despite changes in company risk profile or reinsurer ratings
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Panel rationalisation will improve pricing and counterparty quality

required_data_fields: RI treaty register with cession rate, premium, and recovery data by treaty
Loss triangles and development factors by line of business
Reinsurer credit ratings and panel review history
Gross and net combined ratio by product line (3 years)
IRDAI reinsurance program filing requirements

condition_precedents: Investment management fee benchmark against peers completed
In-house vs. external fund management TCO analysis done
Investment committee approval for mandate changes
industry_sources: IRDAI — Annual Report 2023-24 (irdai.gov.in)
Ardent Partners — AP Metrics That Matter 2024 (ardentpartners.com/ap-metrics-that-matter-in-2025/)
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Audit investment operations: measure settlement fails, reconciliation FTE-hours, custodian fees, and OMS/TMS integration gaps
Rationalise custodian and depository relationships: consolidate to ≤2 custodians per asset class; negotiate fee schedules based on AUM volume
Implement OMS-to-custodian STP for equity and fixed income trade settlement; automate NAV reconciliation and regulatory reporting to IRDAI
Track settlement fail rate, operations cost per portfolio, and regulatory reporting accuracy monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Trade settlement fail rate >1% of settled trades, generating penalty and manual investigation cost
diagnostic_signals.confirms: STP and OMS integration will reduce settlement failures

diagnostic_signals.signal: Investment operations FTE >20 with >50% of time on manual reconciliation and report preparation
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation will release FTE capacity and reduce operations cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Custodian and depository fee spend >15 bps of AUM vs peer benchmark <10 bps
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Consolidation and renegotiation will reduce custody cost

required_data_fields: Investment operations FTE count and time allocation by activity
Custodian fee schedule and AUM by custodian
Settlement fail rate and penalty data
OMS and custodian integration architecture
IRDAI investment reporting requirements

condition_precedents: Actuarial modelling platform (Prophet, Moses, R/Python) upgraded
Model risk management framework updated for automated outputs
Regulator / auditor acceptance of automated reserve computation confirmed
industry_sources: IRDAI — Annual Report 2023-24 (irdai.gov.in)
Gartner — IT Budget Benchmarks 2024 (gartner.com/en/information-technology/insights/it-budget)
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Inventory actuarial models by type (reserving, pricing, capital modelling) and technology (Moses/Prophet/R/Excel); identify manual bottlenecks in production run
Migrate legacy models from Excel to actuarial modelling platform (Prophet, R, Python); establish version control and model validation governance
Automate quarterly reserve production run and IRDAI reporting; implement parallel run for one cycle to validate output
Track actuarial production run time, model error rate, and FTE release from automation monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Quarterly reserve production run takes >3 weeks of actuary time due to manual model execution and reconciliation
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation will accelerate close and free senior actuarial capacity

diagnostic_signals.signal: Actuarial models >50% in Excel with no version control or audit trail
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Platform migration will reduce model risk and improve auditability

diagnostic_signals.signal: IRDAI actuarial filing requires manual preparation consuming >50 actuary-hours per quarter
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation will reduce compliance cost and risk of restatement

required_data_fields: Actuarial model inventory by type and technology
Production run timeline and FTE-hours by model type
IRDAI actuarial reporting requirements and schedule
IT infrastructure for actuarial modelling platform
Model validation and governance framework
